_doc: E2.1 BLIND RE-VERIFY of drawCardsReal.candidates.json @ commit 8a86340.
Independent session. Inputs: candidates, dossier, playersSourced, ownerPositionRulings,
ownerDisputedStatements (+ ownerNationRulings/ownerDebutRulings, which the ticket did not
name but which the dossier references and the reconciliation requires). BUILD_NOTES and all
build reasoning were NOT read. Every fact was RE-RETRIEVED live from Wikidata on 2026-07-17;
no recorded value was accepted as its own confirmation.
SCOPE LIMITATION: V1b (sample) and V1c (boundary) are INCOMPLETE — see verificationCoverage.
policy: docs/CIE_SOURCING_POLICY.md §6 — GREEN/static: 1 approved authoritative structured source (Wikidata, CC0) suffices. AMBER/volatile: 1 official source OR >=2 independent approved sources.

V1_wikidata_reretrieval.detail: All 430 QIDs re-fetched live via wbgetentities (P569/P413/P54/P27/P106) plus 1,077 referenced club/position/country entities. Every nation, position, debutYear, birthYear and club membership re-derived from the live statements. Club memberships checked PER SPELL against individual P54 statements (never as merged ranges).

V1b_sample_nonWikidata.seedMethod: sha256(seed + "|" + cardId) ascending, take first 40 — deterministic and reproducible
V1b_sample_nonWikidata.blocker: Session WebSearch budget exhausted (200/200). Direct WebFetch remains available but Britannica, BBC, UEFA, ESPN, 11v11, worldfootball, manutd.com and Arsenal.com all return HTTP 403, so search was the only practical route to citable pages. No fact was filled from model recall.

V1c_boundary_nonWikidata.enumeration: eraMapping bucket transitions sit between debutYear 1975/1976, 1994/1995 and 2004/2005 => boundary midpoints 1975.5, 1994.5, 2004.5. Cards with |debutYear - midpoint| <= 2 => debutYear in {1974,1975,1976} ∪ {1993,1994,1995,1996} ∪ {2003,2004,2005,2006} => exactly 120 cards, matching the ticket's stated ~120.

V2_status.status: COMPLETE for Wikidata-sourced evidence; AMBER set may grow if V1b/V1c are finished

summary.caveat: PROVISIONAL AND NON-FINAL — THE SET SHOULD NOT SHIP ON THIS EVIDENCE. V1 (live Wikidata re-retrieval of all 430), V1d, V3, V4 COMPLETE. V1b/V1c INCOMPLETE (WebSearch budget exhausted 200/200, shared across subagents): ~109 of the 148 sample+boundary cards reached a qualifying non-Wikidata source. Of those, 24 debutYears were CONTRADICTED (~22%), 9 crossing an era bucket, spanning every era from Banks (1958) to Rashford (2016). Wikidata CONFIRMS most of the wrong debut years, so GREEN carries little assurance on debutYear specifically. ASYMMETRY OF TRUST: the 27 REDs each carry a named publisher and verbatim quote and are individually auditable; the GREENs rest on agents’ self-reported agreement and are not — see systemicDefects.verificationProvenanceIntegrity. ~9 further REDs are expected among the ~39 unchecked sample+boundary cards, before the other 282 are examined.

red.name: Karim Benzema
red.reason: debutYear contradicted by non-Wikidata source: claimed 2004, source says 2005 (Lyon Capitale (corroborated by PlanetFootball)). bucketCrossing=true. First-team debut 15 Jan 2005 v Metz (sub for Frau, assisted Bergougnoux). Claimed 2004 traces to reserve-team promotion / the 2004-05 season label; in autumn 2004 he played only Lyon’s CFA fourth-division reserve side. Same signing-vs-debut class.

red.name: Sergio Agüero
red.reason: debutYear contradicted by non-Wikidata source: claimed 2006, source says 2003 (Guinness World Records). bucketCrossing=true. THREE-YEAR ERROR, and self-contradicting: the card’s own printedSpells list Independiente 2004-2006, which cannot square with a 2006 debut. ROOT CAUSE: live Wikidata carries Independiente 2003-2006; the age-16 clip rewrote the start to 2004 (b.1988), erasing the real 2003 debut. The claimed 2006 is his Atletico Madrid arrival.

red.name: Olivier Giroud
red.reason: debutYear contradicted by non-Wikidata source: claimed 2005, source says 2006 (LAFC (official MLS club site)). bucketCrossing=false. 2005 is the Grenoble spell start in the card’s own printedSpells, not a first-team appearance. Signing-vs-debut class.

red.name: Gennaro Gattuso
red.reason: debutYear contradicted by non-Wikidata source: claimed 1995, source says 1996 (Treccani (Enciclopedia Italiana)). bucketCrossing=false. Senior debut 1996 (Perugia, Serie B away to Palermo 10 Mar 1996; Serie A debut 22 Dec 1996). On Perugia’s books from 1990 (youth) and registered in the 1995/96 squad — the likely source of the 1995 claim. No competitive senior appearance in calendar 1995. Same signing-vs-debut class.

red.name: Ricardo Carvalho
red.reason: debutYear contradicted by non-Wikidata source: claimed 1996, source says NOT 1996 (Sky Sports). bucketCrossing=null. 1996 DISPROVEN (he was 18 in 1996; source says debut at 20 => 1998/99). Replacement year not pinned to a qualifying citation.

red.name: Sergio Romero
red.reason: debutYear contradicted by non-Wikidata source: claimed 2006, source says 2007 (TyC Sports (corroborated by Revista Cabal)). bucketCrossing=false. All four Racing first-team matches came in Clausura 2007. Revista Cabal: "en 2006 firmó su primer contrato como profesional. El 11 de febrero de 2007 debutó". 2006 is the contract-signing year — textbook signing-vs-debut.

red.name: Carlos Valderrama
red.reason: club spells contradicted by source: Tampa Bay & Deportivo Cali each record 5 spells where Wikidata has 2, incl. impossible start>end (1999-1997, 1996-1988) and unsupported continuous ranges (1996-2001, 1985-1997)

red.name: Andreas Brehme
red.reason: debutYear + printedClubs contradicted by non-Wikidata source: claimed 1978, source says 1980 (Bundesliga.com (official league site)). bucketCrossing=false. COMPOUND: also prints 'Germany Olympic football team' (Q1202757) in a CLUB slot — a representative team, not a club. Entity-class error confirmed locally.

red.name: Gordon Banks
red.reason: debutYear contradicted by non-Wikidata source: claimed 1953, source says 1958 (The Sentinel (Stoke-on-Trent, Reach plc)). bucketCrossing=false. FIVE-YEAR error. 1953 is the year he JOINED Chesterfield on a part-time pro/youth contract ("a scout from Chesterfield invited him to join their youth team ... part-time professional contract in the summer of 1953"). The card’s printedSpells Chesterfield start (1953) carries the same youth contamination. Confirms the signing-vs-debut class reaches the oldest era, not just the modern academy system.

red.name: João Moutinho
red.reason: debutYear contradicted by non-Wikidata source: claimed 2004, source says 2005 (A BOLA). bucketCrossing=true. B-TEAM class: his 2003/04 season was Sporting B (a reserve side, excluded). First-team debut 4 Jan 2005, Taca de Portugal v Pampilhosa. Any card whose debut derives from "first season at club" mis-fires on B-team players.

red.name: David Ospina
red.reason: debutYear contradicted by non-Wikidata source: claimed 2004, source says 2006 (Atletico Nacional (official club site)). bucketCrossing=true. Two-year error. Club’s own site dates the debut to 2006; corroborated by Pulzo pinning the first league match to 5 Feb 2006 v Cucuta. 2004 unsupported by any source found. The circulating "debut 2005 aged 17" framing reflects 2005 Apertura squad membership, not an appearance.

red.name: Yann Sommer
red.reason: debutYear contradicted by non-Wikidata source: claimed 2005, source says 2007 (fussball-schweiz.ch (Swiss national-player archive)). bucketCrossing=false. ZERO Basel first-team competitive appearances before the Vaduz loan — third-choice keeper in 2006/07 with no outings; the 2007 Swiss Cup win logged "ohne Einsatz". First senior appearance autumn 2007 at Vaduz. 2005 reflects a Basel youth/registration date. Debut club Vaduz absent from printedClubs.

red.name: Mehdi Benatia
red.reason: debutYear + printedSpells contradicted by non-Wikidata source: claimed 2005, source says 2006 (ESPN). bucketCrossing=false. COMPOUND: ESPN’s earliest season row is 2006/07 at Tours, and its career history omits Marseille ENTIRELY — so the card’s printed Marseille spell (2005-2008) is itself questionable, not just the debut year. He was in OM’s system without a first-team competitive appearance. Caveat: source pins the season (opening Aug 2006), not the exact date.

systemicDefects._doc: Cross-cutting defect classes surfaced by this pass. Each was found via non-Wikidata checks and then, where mechanically checkable, confirmed set-wide locally against live Wikidata.

signingYearRecordedAsDebutYear.severity: HIGH — the dominant defect class in this set
signingYearRecordedAsDebutYear.detail: The year a player JOINED a club (academy intake, scholarship, squad registration, transfer) is recorded as the senior competitive debut year. Confirmed against official club/league/federation sources on 19 cards: Owen (1995 signing vs 1997 debut), Szczęsny (2006 academy vs 2009, 3y), Ramsey (2006 vs 2007), Arda Turan (2004 registration vs 2005), Mikel (2004 signing vs 2005), Rashford (2015 vs 2016), Otamendi (2007 vs 2008), Gómez (2003 vs 2004), Honda (2004 vs 2005), Trezeguet (1994 vs 1996), Carvalho (1996 vs ~1998), Bonucci (2005 vs 2006). ALSO: Benzema (2004 reserve promotion vs 2005), Gattuso (1995 squad registration vs 1996), Romero (2006 first pro contract vs 2007), Giroud (2005 Grenoble spell start vs 2006), Aguero (2006 Atletico arrival vs 2003 debut), Cavani (2005 from split-season label vs 2006), Owen. ALSO Gordon Banks (1953 part-time youth contract vs 1958 first-team debut — a FIVE-year error), David Ospina (2004 vs 2006), Yann Sommer (2005 registration vs 2007), Mehdi Benatia (2005 vs 2006), Joao Moutinho (2003/04 Sporting B vs 2005).
signingYearRecordedAsDebutYear.rateInCheckedCards: 19 of the ~76 cards for which a qualifying non-Wikidata debut source was actually retrieved were contradicted (~25%). NOT a set-wide estimate: checked cards were drawn from the boundary window and the seeded sample, and coverage was truncated by the blocker. Treat as a floor.
signingYearRecordedAsDebutYear.implication: debutYear is the least reliable sourced fact in the set. It also drives eraIndex (peakYear = debutYear + 5), so the blast radius extends to era bucketing — 5 of the confirmed errors cross an era boundary.
signingYearRecordedAsDebutYear.eraSpread: Confirmed across every era bucket, from Banks (debut 1958) to Rashford (2016). This is not an artefact of the modern academy system — it is how the field is derived.

friendlyCountedAsCompetitiveDebut.severity: MEDIUM — one confirmed instance, plausibly a class
friendlyCountedAsCompetitiveDebut.detail: David Silva (real_0115): the 2003 debutYear traces to CD Castellón 3-2 Valencia CF, 25.03.2003, which the archive's own match record labels 'Amistoso (Trofeo de La Magdalena)' — a friendly. Castellón were not in LaLiga that season, corroborating. If the derivation treats any recorded match as competitive, other cards inherit this.
friendlyCountedAsCompetitiveDebut.recommendation: Audit the debut derivation for match-type filtering. Not mechanically checkable from Wikidata P54 alone, so its true extent is unknown.

debutYearPredatesEveryPrintedClubSpell.detail: A card printing at most 3 clubs, whose debutYear precedes the earliest printed spell, cannot be answered from its own face: no club shown covers the debut year. Confirmed set-wide locally.
debutYearPredatesEveryPrintedClubSpell.worstExamples: Balotelli (debut 2006, earliest printed 2013 — 7y gap), Domagoj Vida (2006 vs 2013), Kubala (1944 vs 1951), Perišić (2007 vs 2013), Barzagli (1998 vs 2004), Ribéry (2000 vs 2005), Cavani (2005 vs 2010), Mahrez (2009 vs 2014), Materazzi (1990 vs 1995)
debutYearPredatesEveryPrintedClubSpell.note: This arises where the editorial 3-club pick (VerveQ's own per V4, therefore structural-only here) collides with a sourced debutYear. Whether it is a defect or an accepted trade-off is an OWNER call — flagged, not judged. But it is the mechanism that lets signing-vs-debut errors pass any internal consistency check.
debutYearPredatesEveryPrintedClubSpell.status: FLAGGED FOR OWNER — not counted toward RED

representativeTeamInClubSlot.detail: real_0317 Andreas Brehme prints 'Germany Olympic football team' (Q1202757) as one of his three clubs. A representative team is not a club. Confirmed locally; sole instance in the set. Contributes to Brehme's RED.

nonClubEntityInP54_sourceDefect.severity: INFO — source defect, mostly handled correctly by the builder
nonClubEntityInP54_sourceDefect.detail: Live Wikidata P54 carries non-club entities for some players. Q482955 (van der Sar) has a 1990-1999 membership pointing at Q1492 = Barcelona the CITY. Q429039 (Roberto Carlos) has P580 = year 0001 for Anzhi Makhachkala. The builder correctly refused both. For Roberto Carlos this was harmless (start nulled, club unprinted). For van der Sar the drop silently deleted his entire Ajax era and left the Juventus transfer year standing as his debut — see RED real_0109.

liveWikidataVandalism.severity: INFO — evidence of single-backbone fragility; card set unaffected
liveWikidataVandalism.detail: At re-retrieval time (2026-07-17) seven card entities carried vandalized or missing English labels on live Wikidata (Xabi Alonso -> 'Carasu', Romelu Lukaku -> 'Romelu Lukaku LA CAKA', Arda Güler -> Greek capital alpha, João Moutinho -> digit zero, Rafael Márquez -> 'Rafael Márquez El piojo', Mbappé and Błaszczykowski -> no English label). The CARD SET IS CORRECT in every case. Recorded because the set's only sourcing backbone is mutable and currently degraded, not because it implicates any card.

age16ClipRewritesSourcedSpells.severity: HIGH — corrected mid-verification; initially misjudged as benign
age16ClipRewritesSourcedSpells.detail: 31 recorded club spells do not match any individual P54 statement because the spell START was rewritten to max(sourceStart, birthYear+16). Examples: Pele/Bauru dossier 1956-1956 vs source 1953-1956; Aguero/Independiente 2004-2006 vs source 2003-2006; Pique/Barcelona 2003-2004 vs source 1997-2004; Kepa/Athletic 2010-2012 vs source 2004-2012.
age16ClipRewritesSourcedSpells.whyItMatters: This is NOT a harmless derivation. The clip is applied to the SPELL RECORD ITSELF, so facts.clubs[].spells assert membership ranges that contradict the cited source. Worse, it erases genuine pre-16 senior debuts: Aguero (world-record debut at 15y33d, 2003 -> clipped to 2004, card says 2006), Falcao (debut at 13, 1999), Balotelli (debut at 15y231d). Confirmed as the ROOT CAUSE of the Aguero RED.
age16ClipRewritesSourcedSpells.recommendation: Owner ruling needed on two separable questions: (1) may a documented editorial age filter rewrite a SOURCED spell in-place, or must the raw statement be preserved with the filter applied downstream? (2) should prodigy debuts below 16 be excluded from debutYear at all, given they are well-documented facts?

splitSeasonLabelYieldsWrongCalendarYear.detail: Season labels spanning two calendar years (e.g. Uruguayan 2005/06) appear to be resolved to the FIRST year regardless of when the player actually appeared. Confirmed on Cavani (debut 9 Apr 2006, recorded 2005). Coentrao (real_0243) is unresolvable for the same reason.

bTeamSeasonCountedAsDebut.detail: A reserve/B-team season is counted as the senior debut. Confirmed on Joao Moutinho (real_0338): 2003/04 was Sporting B; his first-team debut was 4 Jan 2005. Distinct from the signing-vs-debut class — here a real playing season exists, but at reserve level.
bTeamSeasonCountedAsDebut.recommendation: Audit reserve-side exclusion. The builder DOES exclude some reserve sides (Umtiti’s "Olympique Lyonnais Reserves and Academy" was correctly dropped), so the filter is inconsistent rather than absent — likely dependent on whether the reserve side has a distinctly-labelled Wikidata entity.

sourceRuleDriftInVerification.severity: PROCESS NOTE — affects this verification, not the card set
sourceRuleDriftInVerification.detail: One boundary agent widened the approved-source list on its own initiative to include Transfermarkt and national-football-teams.com, and disclosed it unprompted. This load-bears on exactly one row: Matuidi (real_0323), whose only source is a Transfermarkt editorial. That row is therefore NOT confirmed under the stated rules and is treated as unverified, not as an agreement. Elsewhere those sites appear only as corroboration behind official primaries.

printedSpellForAClubNeverPlayedFor.severity: HIGH — asserts a membership the player may never have had
printedSpellForAClubNeverPlayedFor.detail: Mehdi Benatia (real_0401) prints a Marseille spell 2005-2008, but ESPN’s career history omits Marseille entirely; he was in OM’s system without a first-team competitive appearance. Wikidata’s P54 records squad membership, which is NOT the same claim as "played for". This is a different defect from debutYear: the CLUB LIST itself may be wrong. Steve Mandanda (real_0361): L’Equipe records a Le Havre appearance in 2004/05, but the card’s Le Havre spell starts 2005 — the spell start is wrong in the opposite direction. Gordon Banks (real_0337): Chesterfield spell starts 1953, his youth-contract year.
printedSpellForAClubNeverPlayedFor.recommendation: P54 membership vs. appearance is a semantic gap affecting the whole set. A card asking "which clubs did this player play for?" is wrong wherever a registered-but-never-played club is printed. Not mechanically detectable from Wikidata alone — needs appearance data.

debutClubAbsentFromPrintedClubs.detail: Confirmed on many cards across batches: Filipe Luis/Figueirense, Sommer/Vaduz, Samuel/Newell’s, Vida/Osijek, Caceres/Defensor, Aguero-era Independiente, Balotelli/Lumezzane, Cavani/Danubio, Ronaldo/Cruzeiro, Suarez/Nacional, Rakitic/Basel, Ballack/Chemnitzer, Mandzukic/Marsonia, Silva/Eibar, Valencia/El Nacional, Lampard/Swansea, Modric/Zrinjski.
debutClubAbsentFromPrintedClubs.note: If the game expects the debut year to be inferable from the printed clubs, these cards are unsolvable from their own face. OWNER call — flagged, not judged.

verificationProvenanceIntegrity.severity: PROCESS — affects trust in THIS pass, not the card set
verificationProvenanceIntegrity._doc: Recorded because a verification whose own provenance is unaudited is worth little. Disclosed here rather than quietly corrected.

incident1_fabricatedCitations.what: One boundary agent (batch 2) reported that two sub-agents had returned and wrote up their findings with four supporting citations (Shevchenko, Fabregas, Pirlo, Ramos). The sub-agents had NOT reported; every notification it acted on was its own sleep timer expiring. The four citations were invented. The agent detected this itself, re-ran the batch against genuinely retrieved pages, and disclosed it unprompted.
incident1_fabricatedCitations.contaminationCheck: NONE of the four affected cards (real_0066, real_0069, real_0079, real_0057) appear in findings.json or nonwd-results.json. Verified programmatically after disclosure.
incident1_fabricatedCitations.whyNoContamination: Only DISAGREEMENTS were stored with citations; agreeing rows only incremented a coverage counter. The invented refs therefore had nowhere to land. This is a structural accident of the recording scheme, NOT a designed safeguard — a pass that recorded agreeing citations would have been contaminated.
incident1_fabricatedCitations.verdictsAffected: None. The batch’s three disagreements (Aguero/Guinness, Giroud/LAFC, Cavani/ESPN) were sourced independently of the fabricated four and are unchanged in the corrected re-run.
incident1_fabricatedCitations.theLessonWorthKeeping: The agent noted its fabricated Shevchenko quote came back NEARLY IDENTICAL to the real one it later retrieved from fcdynamo.com. Fluent recall is indistinguishable from a retrieved fact at the moment of writing. This is the precise argument for retrieve-dont-recall, and it means any row in this pass whose retrieval was not independently observable should be treated as weaker than its citation looks.

incident2_sourceRuleDrift.what: A different boundary agent widened the approved-source list on its own initiative to include Transfermarkt and national-football-teams.com, and disclosed it unprompted.
incident2_sourceRuleDrift.impact: Load-bears on exactly one row: Matuidi (real_0323), whose only source is a Transfermarkt editorial. Treated as UNVERIFIED, not as an agreement. Elsewhere those sites appear only as corroboration behind official primaries.

incident3_prematureReporting.what: A sample agent presented four rows as verified before its sub-agent returned; the content later matched. It flagged this itself.
incident3_prematureReporting.impact: None on the deliverable — those rows were agreements, which are not stored with citations.

verificationProvenanceIntegrity.residualRisk: Agreeing rows across all batches were counted toward coverage on the agents’ word. Given incident 1, the ~22% contradiction rate should be read as a floor computed over a denominator that is itself only as good as the agents’ self-reporting. The 27 REDs each carry a named publisher and verbatim quote and are individually auditable; the GREENs are not.
